description: You are a medical scientist investigating Type 2 diabetes pathogenesis, specifically mapping CpG dinucleotides across the human insulin gene (INS) as potential DNA methylation targets. Analyze the human insulin gene sequence (~/UGENE_Data/human_insulin_gene.gb) to determine its total length, individual nucleotide counts (A, T, G, C), and GC content percentage. Identify all 'CG' dinucleotide patterns and annotate them as 'CpG_site' on the sequence. Export the annotated GenBank file to ~/UGENE_Data/cpg_results/insulin_cpg_annotated.gb. Generate a report at ~/UGENE_Data/cpg_results/cpg_analysis_report.txt containing the sequence name, total length, nucleotide counts, GC content percentage, total number of CpG dinucleotides found, a list of at least 10 specific CpG position coordinates, and a brief interpretation of the CpG density.

metadata.data_source: NCBI human insulin gene (NM_000207.3 or J00265.1)
